name: FAB Cashback Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1% on other eligible retail spend; 0.15% on select low-interchange categories (utilities, government, education, real estate, telecom)

primary_uses: Statement credit via FAB Mobile app; 100 FAB Rewards = AED 1 (10,000 FAB Rewards = AED 100)

benefits: Airport lounge access subject to Mastercard conditions
Cinema and dining offers

name: FAB Rewards Indulge Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1 FAB Reward per AED 1 on all eligible spend, including international spend

categories.rate: 20,000 bonus FAB Rewards when eligible monthly spend reaches AED 10,000

primary_uses: Cashback, travel and retail rewards
Current official cashback calculator: 66,800 points = AED 200, approximately AED 0.002994 per point

benefits: No annual fee
Monthly spend bonus

name: Emirates NBD U by Emaar Signature Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1.25% back in UPoints on eligible non-Emaar spend (10 UPoints = AED 1)

primary_uses: Redeem at participating Emaar hotels, malls, entertainment and other U by Emaar partners

benefits: U by Emaar lifestyle privileges
Cinema benefit subject to spend conditions
data_caveat: UPoints redeem at a fixed 10 UPoints = AED 1 at partner locations. The 5X accelerator (6.25% back) covers Emaar Malls, Emaar Hospitality Group and Emaar Entertainment venues; community supermarkets earn on lower retail tiers.

name: Emirates NBD dnata World Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1.5% back in dnata Points on eligible domestic and international spend, capped at 3,000 dnata Points per statement cycle

primary_uses: Redeem through dnata for flights, hotels, holidays and travel services

benefits: Travel and lifestyle benefits
Airport lounge access subject to Mastercard conditions
data_caveat: 1 dnata Point = AED 1. Standard domestic/international non-partner retail is capped at 3,000 points per statement cycle; partner brackets (dnata Travel and partner stores) are bound to an independent 20,000 points per calendar year.

name: Emirates NBD Duo Credit Cards

fees.waiver_conditions: AED 700 standard annual fee; currently waived under a promotional introductory offer

rewards.base_rate: 0.5% back in Plus Points on other eligible spend (1 Plus Point = AED 1)

primary_uses: Cash credit at AED 0.75 per Plus Point, minimum 500 points, under the current official redemption schedule

benefits: Two-card proposition
Cinema and lounge benefits subject to conditions
data_caveat: 1 Plus Point = AED 1. Capped at 2,000 Plus Points per statement cycle across the card bundle.

name: Emirates NBD Visa Flexi Credit Card

fees.waiver_conditions: No joining or annual fee under the current product offer

rewards.base_rate: 1.5% back in Plus Points on general eligible spend (1 Plus Point = AED 1)

primary_uses: Cash credit at AED 0.75 per Plus Point, minimum 500 points, under the current official redemption schedule

benefits: Customisable lifestyle benefits; customisation does not change the reward earn rate
Customisable lifestyle benefits; a fee of AED 99 per service applies if monthly spend is below AED 5,000

name: ADCB 365 Cashback Credit Card

eligibility.employer_restrictions: AED 5,000 salary or AED 3,000 minimum assigned credit limit

fees.waiver_conditions: First year free; AED 383.25 including VAT from the second year

rewards.base_rate: 1% on other eligible local spend; 1% on non-EU international spend; 0.5% on EU and selected categories

primary_uses: Automatic statement cashback under ADCB cashback programme rules

benefits: First-year annual fee waiver
Lifestyle and Mastercard benefits
data_caveat: Category rates reflect the July 2026 restructure: groceries reduced to 3%, fuel and Salik increased to 5%, AI/digital subscriptions codified at 5%. Total cashback is bound by the AED 1,000 monthly overall cap.

name: ADCB Traveller Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1.5% on other eligible domestic AED spend; international spend earns at standard domestic rates with 0% foreign transaction fees

benefits: Travel insurance
Airport lounge access subject to Mastercard conditions
data_caveat: The current official product page confirms reward rates, caps, salary and age but does not expose the annual or joining fee amount in page text. No fee was inferred.

name: Mashreq Cashback Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 1% on eligible local retail spend; 1% on eligible international spend (up to 2% during promotional periods)

primary_uses: Statement credit, minimum AED 100 redemption; unredeemed cashback is forfeited after 36 months of inactivity

benefits: No annual fee
No minimum monthly spend to earn
data_caveat: Mashreq publishes the general local rate only as up to 1% and does not expose the underlying local tier table on the current product page. The raw official wording is preserved.

name: Mashreq Platinum Plus Credit Card

rewards.base_rate: 2 Vantage points per AED 1 on eligible international and other local spend

primary_uses: Redeem through Mashreq Vantage for cashback, travel and merchant rewards

benefits: Accelerated supermarket, fuel and dining earn

name: HSBC Live+ Credit Card

eligibility.employer_restrictions: AED 5,000 salary for employees of HSBC-approved companies; otherwise AED 12,500

fees.waiver_conditions: First year free; later annual fee waived when annual spend is at least AED 12,000

rewards.base_rate: 0.5% on other eligible spend and on all eligible spend when the AED 3,000 monthly threshold is not met

primary_uses: Automatically credited monthly to the card account

benefits: Dining, fuel, grocery and entertainment cashback
Complimentary 12-month Careem Plus subscription
Discounts at partner restaurants

name: HSBC Max Rewards Credit Card

fees.waiver_conditions: First year free; later annual fee waived when annual spend is at least AED 12,000

rewards.base_rate: 1 point per AED 1; 2 points per AED 1 when monthly eligible spend exceeds AED 3,000

primary_uses: Flights, hotels, retail rewards and statement credit options vary by redemption
HSBC does not publish one universal AED-per-point value; a current Premier illustration states up to AED 2,350 for 330,000 points, approximately AED 0.00712 only at that offer maximum

benefits: Servicing information retained for existing cardholders
data_caveat: The card is not available for new applications. The current servicing page does not publish application eligibility. The reward value is redemption-dependent, so no fixed value is asserted. Minimum salary (AED 12,500) and minimum age (21) are inferred from HSBC UAE Signature-tier requirements; the official page for this legacy product does not publish them.

name: Emirates Islamic Flex Elite Credit Card

fees.waiver_conditions: First-year fee waived under the welcome promotion; second-year and later waiver requires AED 100,000 annual retail spend

rewards.base_rate: 2.5 EI SmartMiles per AED 1 on eligible domestic spend

primary_uses: Travel, instant purchase redemption and partner conversions
Official examples show 75,000 miles = AED 750 and 100,000 miles = AED 1,000, so AED 0.01 per SmartMile for those redemptions; minimum travel redemption 4,000 miles

benefits: Customisable lifestyle privileges; reward earning itself follows the fixed official schedule
data_caveat: The current official product and earn pages do not publish the application salary or annual membership amount. Those fields are null rather than guessed.

name: Emirates Islamic Amazon World Credit Card

eligibility.employer_restrictions: World card is for monthly income above AED 15,000; lower eligible income receives the Platinum variant

rewards.base_rate: Prime members: up to 2% on eligible domestic non-Amazon spend and up to 2.5% on eligible international spend; non-Prime members: 1% on each

primary_uses: Use at Amazon.ae checkout; 1 point = AED 1; points expire after five years

benefits: No annual fee
No published reward earning limit
data_caveat: The official page uses up to wording because eligible-category treatment applies. The raw wording is retained instead of assuming every transaction earns the maximum.

name: DIB Consumer Cashback Platinum Card

fees.waiver_conditions: AED 249 excluding VAT; current limited offer waives the first-year fee

rewards.base_rate: No general base cashback published

primary_uses: Statement cashback under DIB cashback rules

benefits: Category cashback across common UAE household spending
data_caveat: Cashback is a shared pool of up to 4% across everyday household categories, not an independent 4% per category. If billing-cycle spend falls below AED 4,000, all cashback earned in that statement cycle is forfeited entirely.

name: DIB Consumer Cashback Reward Card

fees.waiver_conditions: AED 200 excluding VAT; current limited offer waives the first-year fee

rewards.base_rate: No general base cashback published

primary_uses: Statement cashback under DIB cashback rules

benefits: Category cashback across common UAE household spending
data_caveat: Cashback is a shared pool of up to 3% across combined everyday household categories. If billing-cycle spend falls below AED 3,000, all cashback earned in that statement cycle is forfeited entirely.

name: ADIB Cashback Visa Covered Card

benefits: Cashback in five daily-spend groups

benefits: Visa Infinite travel and lifestyle benefits
data_caveat: All transactions executed within the European Economic Area earn zero Wala'a Rewards; DIB strips point generation on EEA spend to offset interchange margins.

excluded_spend.reason: DIB pays zero Wala'a Rewards on European Economic Area transactions. Our spend taxonomy has no EEA segment (EEA spend is a subset of 'international'), so this models the conservative bound: ALL international spend on this card earns nothing. Non-EEA foreign spend really would earn 3.5/AED, so this understates the card. Needs a distinct EEA spend segment to model exactly.
